articles.author: Jon Gambrell, Samy Magdy
articles.description: Iran and the United States have reached an impasse again over how to end their war. Their ceasefire is growing increasingly shaky, with the two sides exchanging fire in recent days, ships and Gulf states being targeted, and fighting flaring between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah. The volatility could tip the Middle East back into open warfare and prolong the worldwide energy crisis sparked by the conflict. President Donald Trump is expected to use a trip this week to China to urge Chinese President Xi Jinping to pressure Iran into making concessions and end the current limbo. But getting to any deal likely remains tough work.
articles.title: The Iran war is at an impasse ahead of Trump's trip to China

articles.author: Bridget Brown
articles.description: Iran and the United States reached an impasse again Monday over how to end their war while their ceasefire grew increasingly shaky ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's trip to China.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump rejects Iran's latest response to ceasefire proposal ahead of his trip to China

articles.author: Chan Ho-him
articles.description: Asian stocks are mixed Monday after Wall Street set more records. Oil prices have surged more than 4% following U.S. President Donald Trump’s rejection of Tehran’s response to the latest U.S. proposal on ending the war in Iran. U.S. futures edged lower. Markets in South Korea and Japan hit an intraday high. With Brent crude trading at above $105 a barrel, analysts say resolving supply chain disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz remains the key. The Iran war is also set to be part of the discussion when Trump meets Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing later this week.
articles.title: Oil jumps 4% and Asian shares are mixed after Wall Street set records

articles.author: Vahid Salemi
articles.description: Iran responded Sunday to the latest U.S. ceasefire proposal, but President Donald Trump rejected it as “TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!”
articles.title: Photos of life inside Iran after latest US proposal falters

articles.author: Will Weissert
articles.description: President Donald Trump's trip to China may not come with the same fanfare that accompanied his first-term visit. In 2017, China went all out to welcome the new American president. For this week's get-together between Trump and China's Xi Jinping, the Iran war is raising the stakes for both sides. For one, China is the biggest buyer of Iran's oil. Trump, meanwhile, had hoped China would do more to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz. But Trump has predicted a warm welcome from Xi, even expecting to be offered a “big, fat hug.”
articles.title: Trump sees a 'big, fat hug' from China's Xi, but Iran war adds trip tension

articles.author: Aamer Madhani, Simina Mistreanu
articles.description: President Donald Trump has demonstrated greater ambivalence toward Taiwan in his second term. That's raising questions about his commitment to maintaining American support for the self-ruled island ahead of next week's summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. In December, Trump authorized an $11 billion arms package for Taiwan but delayed its delivery and discussed the sale with Xi. He's criticized Taiwan for “stealing” America's semiconductor business and pressured them to invest in U.S. tech sectors. Analysts say Xi might use the high-profile moment to urge Trump to loosen ties with Taiwan, which Beijing views as its breakaway province.
articles.title: Trump-Xi summit has high stakes for Taiwan

articles.author: Kanis Leung
articles.description: As Donald Trump's meeting with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping approaches, the family of Hong Kong's jailed activist Jimmy Lai is hopeful that the U.S. President could help free their father. Lai has been sentenced to 20 years in prison under a Beijing-imposed national security law. His son Sebastien Lai told The Associated Press that if his father dies in prison, it would devastate the family and create a martyr, so it's a lose-lose situation for everyone. While some experts are cautiously optimistic that a diplomatic solution could happen if Trump pushes it hard enough, others are pessimistic because Xi's China prioritizes national security and resists foreign interference.
articles.title: Trump's deal making with Xi may determine Hong Kong jailed activist Jimmy Lai's fate

articles.description: A French woman evacuated from a cruise ship has tested positive for hantavirus and her health worsened in the hospital overnight, French Health Minister Stephanie Rist said Monday. The woman was among five French passengers repatriated Sunday to Paris from the MV Hondius. She developed symptoms on the flight to Paris, Rist told public broadcaster France-Inter. Passengers from the ship began flying home aboard military and government planes Sunday after the vessel anchored in the Canary Islands. The World Health Organization recommended close monitoring of the former passengers, and many countries quarantined them. An American passenger being flown to Nebraska also tested positive but had no symptoms.
articles.title: Another cruise ship passenger tests positive for hantavirus

articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.
articles.title: Onboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship, in photos

articles.author: Mike Stobbe
articles.description: Public health experts are questioning the U.S. government's response to the hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship that involves Americans. President Donald Trump says “we seem to have things under very good control.” But experts and former government health officials say the response by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has been feeble compared with how it dealt with similar outbreaks in the past. They say the CDC’s diminished role in the hantavirus outbreak is an indicator that the agency is no longer the force in international health or the protector of domestic health that it once was.
articles.title: Experts question CDC's response to cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
